title: Stable Merge Sort with Missing Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable merge sort algorithm to sort it in ascending order. Your code should swap elements correctly to ensure the array is sorted.
statement.inputFormat: An integer array of length 1 to 10,000.
statement.notes: The input array may contain duplicate elements.

examples.explanation: The array should be sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Remember to use a stable merge sort algorithm to preserve the order of equal elements.
Pay attention to the swap operation in the merge function.

editorial.title: Stable Merge Sort
editorial.intuition: Stable merge sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm that splits the input array into two halves, recursively sorts each half, and then merges the two sorted halves while preserving the order of equal elements.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force solution would involve using a simple sorting algorithm like bubble sort or selection sort, but this would be inefficient for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a more efficient sorting algorithm like quicksort or heapsort, but the task requires a stable sort.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is a stable merge sort algorithm that correctly swaps elements between the two sorted halves.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the algorithm can be proved by showing that the merge function preserves the order of equal elements and that the base cases are handled correctly.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the algorithm on a sample input can help identify any issues with the code.
